candidates.title: Elections Alberta set to begin verifying signatures on separatist referendum petition
candidates.description: Elections Alberta says it's set to begin verifying the signatures on a petition looking to force a vote on the province separating from Canada.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Convoy leader Tamara Lich attends July 4 party at U.S. ambassador's residence in Ottawa
candidates.description: Convoy protest leader Tamara Lich had to obtain permission from her parole officer to attend an official Independence Day event at the U.S. ambassador's residence in Ottawa. She's serving a conditional sentence after being convicted of mischief in October 2025.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Summer McIntosh's coach hails 'very important' 200m butterfly world record
candidates.description: Legendary swimming coach Bob Bowman has witnessed and coached many athletes to world records over his illustrious career. But being part of his first one while coaching Canadian swimming superstar Summer McIntosh on Sunday night in Montreal felt a little different for the veteran coach.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Alberta pharmacists scramble to keep up as reports of prescription forgeries tick up again
candidates.description: The Alberta College of Pharmacy recently told its members that forgery attempts are on the rise, and it urged pharmacists to notify police.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Some N.L. soccer fans see red after Trump asks for player suspension review
candidates.description: “Who is next? Is the King of England going to withdraw the red card … where does it stop?"
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Saskatchewan village celebrates new beginning without ‘Queen of Canada’ cult
candidates.description: Richmound, Sask., held a street party on Saturday to celebrate starting over after two years living with the Kingdom of Canada cult. They marked the occasion by announcing a year-round greenhouse project.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Iran war drove inflation expectations up, hurt business confidence: BoC
candidates.description: The Iran war both hampered business confidence and sent inflation expectations soaring, the Bank of Canada revealed in a new set of business outlook surveys published Monday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Carney chooses German submarines for Canadian navy fleet
candidates.description: German shipbuilder ThyssenKrupp Marine Systems is Canada's preferred choice to build the navy's new fleet of submarines — a multibillion-dollar defence program that is expected to be the largest in the country's history.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Diageo enters agreement to sell Crown Royal bottling plant in Amherstburg, Ont.
candidates.description: The makers of Crown Royal whiskey have entered into an agreement to sell their former bottling facility in Amherstburg, Ont., Diageo, has confirmed to CBC.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Air Canada halts, delays several flight routes to U.S. destinations amid high fuel costs
candidates.description: Air Canada is once again scaling back flights to the U.S. amid high jet fuel costs and lower demand for trips south of the border. Air Canada, WestJet and Air Transat all made cuts to their summer flight capacity to the U.S. earlier this year as the Iran war sent jet fuel prices soaring.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 6 presumed drowned in charter boat disaster mourned in Richmond, B.C.
candidates.description: About 100 mourners gathered at a public dock in Richmond, B.C., to commemorate six victims missing and feared drowned after a charter boat sinking in the Strait of Georgia a week earlier.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Fatal crash leaves travellers stranded on B.C.'s Sea-to-Sky Highway overnight
candidates.description: Hundreds of people were stranded on British Columbia's Sea-to-Sky Highway overnight, where a motorcycle crash left one woman dead and prompted a lengthy closure.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: German submarine deal 'unfortunate' for Algoma Steel, Sault Ste. Marie, Ont., region defence expert says
candidates.description: The bid for a federal submarine contract that could have provided Algoma Steel in Sault Ste. Marie, Ont., with additional infrastructure and hundreds of new jobs appears unsuccessful. Ottawa is expected to announce German shipbuilder TKMS has won the bid to build the Canadian navy's new fleet of submarines, over South Korea's Hanwha Ocean.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: B.C. MLA Jordan Kealy seeks jury trial for sexual assault charge
candidates.description: Independent MLA Jordan Kealy, who represents the riding of Peace River North, made a brief first appearance in Fort St. John provincial court on Monday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ford, Smith unveil plan for new ‘Northern Shield’ oil pipeline between Alberta and Ontario
candidates.description: The premiers of Alberta and Ontario unveiled a proposed 3,300 km pipeline to transport oil from Western Canada to refineries in Southern Ontario.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Gavin McKenna representing Tr'ondëk Hwëch'in culture at NHL draft 'awe-inspiring' for community
candidates.description: Gavin McKenna was the first Indigenous player from Canada to be the number one NHL draft pick in nearly 50 years. The Tr'ondëk Hwëch'in citizen from Whitehorse has made efforts to represent his culture on the world stage.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Cuba hit with yet another island-wide power outage
candidates.description: Cuba's national electric grid collapsed on Monday at midday, ‌the country's grid operator said, leaving around 10 million people on the Caribbean ​island without power.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Republican bill targets Canada's provincial booze bans
candidates.description: A Republican lawmaker in Washington is firing back at Canadian provinces for retaliating against U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs by banning sales of U.S. alcohol.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Israeli strike kills 4, including school principal, in south Lebanon: Health Ministry
candidates.description: Four people, including a school principal, were killed in an Israeli strike on a vehicle in southern Lebanon on Monday, Lebanon's Health Ministry said, in one of the deadliest attacks in weeks.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Microsoft to cut 4,800 jobs, overhaul Xbox unit
candidates.description: Microsoft said on Monday it would cut 4,800 jobs, or about 2.1 per cent of its global workforce, including overhauling its Xbox gaming business and ‌divesting up to five studios as it looks to boost returns after years of heavy investment in the division.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Spain advances to quarterfinals in Portugal star Ronaldo's potential final World Cup game
candidates.description: Mikel Merino scored seconds into second-half stoppage time, and Spain beat Portugal 1-0 on Monday in Arlington, Texas, possibly the end of superstar Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup career.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Inside Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding: What we know
candidates.description: Weeks of rumours are finally over. Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are officially married. Still, so many details are under wraps. But here's what we've been able to piece together about the couple's big day at the midtown Manhattan arena.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump says he asked FIFA to review the now-rescinded 1-game ban for U.S. forward Balogun
candidates.description: Reaction was still pouring in Monday after FIFA on Sunday ‌suspended American striker Folarin Balogun's red card ban, clearing him to play in Monday night's Round of 16 match with Belgium after U.S. President Donald Trump personally urged FIFA president Gianni Infantino to review the case.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Russian missile and drone attack kills at least 22 people in Ukraine
candidates.description: Russia unleashed waves of missiles and drones at Ukraine early Monday, killing at least 22 people in attacks that exposed widening gaps in country's air defences more than four years into Moscow's full-scale invasion, authorities said.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Venezuelan earthquake survivors make the hard shift from rescue to recovery
candidates.description: Venezuelans say they are largely alone in their search for the dead after the June 24 earthquakes that killed thousands. Here's a visual overview of how Venezuelans are dealing with the search for bodies, and how they're remembering those who have died.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Wildfire in southern France forces some 10,000 people from their homes
candidates.description: A wildfire burning out of control in southwestern France has forced the evacuation of 10,000 people from two dozen small towns and villages near the ‌Spanish border and officials said strong winds on Monday would further fan the blaze.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Prince Harry won't stay at Buckingham Palace during U.K. visit
candidates.description: Prince Harry will not stay at Buckingham Palace during his stay in Britain this week, the palace said, despite a spokesman for the King's second son telling media on Monday that ‌he had accepted an invitation to do so.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Death toll from Venezuela's earthquakes rises to 3,342
candidates.description: The death toll from Venezuela's twin quakes has risen to ‌3,342, according to numbers released by the Venezuelan information ministry ​on Sunday. The new ​tally also puts the number of injured ​at 16,470, while the number of ‌homeless has risen to 17,345.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump targets Obamas again with doctored image posted on social media
candidates.description: U.S. President Donald Trump on Sunday posted a falsified image of former president Barack Obama and his wife, Michelle Obama, waving before boarding an Air Force One aircraft that had been spray-painted with graffiti.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Americans don’t recognize the damage Trump is doing, says novelist John Irving
candidates.description: U.S.-Canadian novelist John Irving says he won’t travel to America to promote his new book, Queen Esther, because of his opposition to President Donald Trump.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Son who succeeded slain Iranian leader Khamenei absent from funeral as 3 other sons appear
candidates.description: The son who succeeded Iran's slain leader wasn't at the funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and four other family members on Sunday, while three other sons showed up and prayed beside the coffins.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Norway headed to World Cup quarterfinals for 1st time after 2-1 win over Brazil
candidates.description: Erling Haaland headed in the go-ahead goal in the 80th minute and scored again before the end of regulation time, carrying Norway into the World Cup quarterfinals for the first time with a 2-1 victory over Brazil on Sunday that
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Venezuelan doctors return home to give aid amid quake devastation
candidates.description: Marianyi Bastidas is one of several doctors and specialists who left their birth country of Venezuela years ago, but returned to help after the disastrous twin earthquakes in late June.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: International Swimming League to pay over $7M US in overdue 2021 prize money, eyes comeback
candidates.description: The International Swimming League will pay more than 300 athletes long-overdue prize money owed from its 2021 season, Commissioner Ben Allen told Reuters, as the professional swimming ‌circuit seeks to rebuild trust and relaunch after years of inactivity.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Rogers to become sole owner of MLSE as Tanenbaum sells remaining stake for $4.35B
candidates.description: The deal to buy the remaining 25 per cent stake in Maple Leaf Sports & Entertainment from Kilmer Sports Inc. means Rogers will own the Raptors, Leafs, Toronto FC, Argos, Marlies and Scotiabank Arena — plus the Blue Jays, Rogers Centre and Sportsnet, which it already owned. While the deal cements the company's dominance of Toronto sports, experts say it won't change anything for fans.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Think you can win on prediction markets? Here's why you're more likely to lose
candidates.description: Prediction markets, based on the idea that anybody can profit from predicting the outcome of an event, are about to make their way to Canada. But research suggests only a fraction of sophisticated users reap the benefits due to their extensive knowledge of finance, economics and computer skills that average users can't compete with.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump confirms he asked Fifa to review Balogun ban
candidates.description: President Donald Trump confirms he asked Fifa to review United States striker Folarin Balogun's one-match suspension at the World Cup.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Charlie Kirk's family attends hearing for suspect charged with his murder
candidates.description: Tyler Robinson, 23, is accused of murdering Kirk, a key Trump ally and founder of youth organisation Turning Point USA.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Ex-Syrian intelligence chief found guilty of torture and sexual abuse by Austrian court
candidates.description: Two former Syrian officials went on trial in Vienna, Austria, accused of crimes against opponents of former Syrian leader Bashar al-Assad
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Huge crowds fill Tehran streets for Khamenei's funeral procession
candidates.description: Many people were waving Iranian flags and red banners symbolising vengeance for the former supreme leader's war death.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: More than 1,000 arrested as part of global human trafficking crackdown
candidates.description: Interpol says the vast majority of the 2,070 victims or potential victims identified were women being trafficked for sex.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'I ate ketchup and cheese,' says Venezuelan girl trapped under quake rubble for 32 hours
candidates.description: Fabiana was trapped in the rubble of a 10-storey residential building after two earthquakes rocked Venezuela in June.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Sri Lanka prison riots leave 26 dead and more than 100 injured
candidates.description: The two days of violence at Negombo Prison are the worst prison riots in the country in years.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Marine Le Pen appeal verdict: Why this moment matters for France
candidates.description: The leader of France's National Rally leads the opinion polls ahead of the 2027 presidential election and will now find out if she can stand.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Thousands of forgotten WW1 soldiers recognised for first time
candidates.description: Nearly 10,000 soldiers' names are being added to the official database of war dead, a cherished step for many of their descendants.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: From 'a beautiful night' to 'world's greatest hangover', guests react to Taylor and Travis's wedding
candidates.description: Some of the famous guests have been posting on social media about the big day in New York.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Is this a breakthrough moment for soccer in the US?
candidates.description: Victory for the national team against Belgium on Monday could mean the sport takes further strides among American audiences.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Why an anti-sacrilege law in India's Punjab has sparked controversy
candidates.description: The dispute has revived one of Punjab's most sensitive political and religious issues ahead of state elections next year.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'The spectacle Iran wants the world to see': Lyse Doucet in Tehran
candidates.description: Huge crowds have lined the streets of Tehran for the funeral procession of Iran's late supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Watch: Russian attack leaves gaping hole in Kyiv residential building
candidates.description: The BBC's Sarah Rainsford is at the scene of one of the blasts to hit Ukraine's capital overnight.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Watch: Why Australia's PM apologised for 'inappropriate' Kylie comments
candidates.description: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has apologised for remarks he made about popstar Kylie Minogue in a podcast interview last week.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Why Ukrainian strikes on annexed Crimea are a blow to Putin
candidates.description: Eastern Europe correspondent Sarah Rainsford explains why Crimea is a target for Kyiv, and why the peninsula matters to Vladimir Putin.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Emotion and politics merge in Tehran at funeral of former supreme leader
candidates.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent Lyse Doucet is in Tehran, where funeral events are taking place in honour of Iran's former leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Will this heatwave feel as hot and sticky as the last one?
candidates.description: The UK's next heatwave is here, but will it feel as hot and sticky as the last one?
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Trump, Mamdani give contrasting July 4th speeches. And, U.S. faces Belgium in World Cup
candidates.description: For the nation's 250th birthday, President Trump and NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani had opposing visions of America. And, the U.S. and Belgium face off today in the FIFA World Cup.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Huge crowds of mourners join a funeral procession for Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
candidates.description: Iran holds a funeral procession for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei more than four months after he was killed in U.S.-Israeli strikes.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Russia's missile and drone attacks on Ukraine kill at least 22
candidates.description: Russia unleashed waves of missiles and drones at Ukraine, killing at least 22 people. The attacks exposed gaps in the country's air defenses more than four years into Moscow's full-scale invasion.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Trump won spending promises from NATO last year. This week, he'll try to enforce them
candidates.description: President Donald Trump is heading to Ankara, Turkey, for the annual NATO summit. Last year, he pushed NATO allies to spend more on defense. This year, his mission is to try to enforce those pledges.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Y si sí: the phrase uniting Mexico during the World Cup
candidates.description: Mexico's World Cup run has inspired a phrase heard across the country: "Y si sí." Or in English: "What if we can?" Anamaria Sayre reports from Mexico City.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: NATO leaders look for unity as Trump attends annual summit
candidates.description: As NATO leaders gather for this week's summit in Turkey, Europe is preparing to shoulder more of its own defense. David M. Cattler of the Center for European Policy Analysis explains why.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Hong Kong's 'sound of silence': 6 years under the national security law
candidates.description: Six years after Beijing imposed its National Security Law, many of Hong Kong's opposition voices have gone silent. Danny Vincent looks at what's changed.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Folarin Balogun's ban is lifted by FIFA, allowing U.S. striker to play Belgium
candidates.description: In an unusual move, FIFA's Disciplinary Committee announced that it would delay Folarin Balogun's one-game red card ban. It came after President Trump spoke to FIFA President Gianni Infantino.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Super Typhoon Bavi brings destruction to Guam and surrounding Pacific islands
candidates.description: People in the Northern Mariana Islands — remote U.S. territories in the Pacific Ocean — are dealing with the aftermath of intense winds and severe flooding from Super Typhoon Bavi.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Hate food waste? 7 creative ways to turn your leftovers into a new meal
candidates.description: We asked our audience to share their favorite go-to recipes for leftovers. Here are seven dishes — like stuffed peppers and a biryani casserole — that can help you use up all your fridge scraps.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Egg producers settle with DOJ, states over price-fixing complaint
candidates.description: Seventeen states and the Department of Justice have settled a case of alleged price-fixing by major egg producers. Egg prices soared in recent years but have since fallen sharply.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Get your reading glasses... in the post office
candidates.description: In some towns in India, people working for a non profit approach people who appear to have trouble reading small print to get them hooked up with glasses.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: These Medicare beneficiaries thought their drug plan was free. Then they lost it
candidates.description: Thousands of people lost coverage over as little as $8 in delinquent payments. They didn't know their zero-dollar premiums had gone up and they owed money. Most now can't get coverage until 2027.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Fast walkers in their 80s cut their risk of cognitive decline by half, a study finds
candidates.description: A new study of people 80 and older with exceptional gaits finds fast walkers have about a 50% lower risk of cognitive decline, showing the connection between physical health and brain health.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Restrictions could be lifted on peptides loved by wellness influencers
candidates.description: Unproven peptides touting wellness have been relegated to a gray market because of Biden-era restrictions. Under the Trump administration, that could change.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Construction is a man's game. These women are demolishing the barriers
candidates.description: Women make up a miniscule 3% of construction workers in Kenya. But a nonprofit group is offering them a way to get into the game ... and to get ahead.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 6 at 5:52PM EDT until July 6 at 6:15PM EDT by NWS Jackson KY
candidates.description: At 552 PM EDT, a severe thunderstorm was located over Glo, or 12 miles northeast of Hindman, moving north at 25 mph. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and quarter size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Hail damage to vehicles is expected. Expect wind damage to roofs, siding, and trees. This severe storm will be near... Estill around 555 PM EDT. Other locations in the path of this severe thunderstorm include Martin.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Flood Advisory issued July 6 at 5:51PM EDT until July 6 at 7:15PM EDT by NWS Charleston SC
candidates.description: * WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected. * WHERE...Berkeley County in southeastern South Carolina... * WHEN...Until 715 PM EDT. * IMPACTS...Overflowing poor drainage areas. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS... - At 551 PM EDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to thunderstorms. Minor flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly in the advisory area. Between 2 and 3 inches of rain have fallen. - Additional rainfall amounts of 1 to 2 inches are expected over the area. This additional rain will result in minor flooding. - Some locations that will experience flooding include... Jamestown, Simmonsville, Palmerville, Newland, Shulerville, Thomas Corner and Farewell Corner.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

Little Rock... North Little Rock... Sherwood... Jacksonville... Lonoke... England... Wrightsville... Keo... Estes... Meto... Protho Junction... Plum Bayou Mounds SP... Sweet Home... McAlmont... candidates.description: SVRLBF The National Weather Service in North Platte has issued a * Severe Thunderstorm Warning for... West central Cherry County in north central Nebraska... * Until 530 PM CDT. * At 450 PM CDT, severe thunderstorms were located along a line extending from 14 miles southwest of Medicine Lake to 28 miles north of Ashby, moving southeast at 10 mph. HAZARD...Ping pong ball size hail and 60 mph wind gusts. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...People and animals outdoors will be injured. Expect hail damage to roofs, siding, windows, and vehicles. Expect wind damage to roofs, siding, and trees. * These severe thunderstorms will remain over mainly rural areas of west central Cherry County. This includes Highway 61 between mile markers 186 and 199.

candidates.description: At 351 PM MDT, Doppler radar was tracking showers over Denver, moving east at 5 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 50 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. This strong thunderstorm will be near... Murphy around 455 PM CDT. Midnight around 500 PM CDT. Louise around 510 PM CDT. Silver City around 515 PM CDT.

candidates.description: SVRSHV The National Weather Service in Shreveport has issued a * Severe Thunderstorm Warning for... Southwestern De Soto Parish in northwestern Louisiana... Northern Shelby County in eastern Texas... Panola County in northeastern Texas... * Until 545 PM CDT. * At 448 PM CDT, a severe thunderstorm was located near Carthage, moving east at 15 mph. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and small hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Expect damage to roofs, siding, and trees. * Locations impacted include... Carthage, Center, Logansport, Tenaha, Timpson, Beckville, Joaquin, Shelbyville, Gary City, Grand Cane, Longstreet, Stanley, Woods, Paxton, Deadwood, James and Fairplay.
